text_excerpt: I would rather die having spoken after my manner, than speak in your manner and live.
label: P1
resistance_rationale: Explicit choice of death over abandoning his manner of speaking. Stakes: execution. The cost could not be higher — life itself. Perfect P1 at maximum resistance.

text_excerpt: I die the King's faithful servant, but God's first.
label: P1
resistance_rationale: Said moments before beheading. Maintains divided loyalty claim (king vs. conscience) at the cost of his life. Stakes unambiguous, holding evident.

text_excerpt: Perhaps you pronounce this sentence against me with greater fear than I receive it.
label: P1
resistance_rationale: Said to the Inquisition upon receiving a death sentence. No capitulation, direct confrontation of the judges. The cost is death by burning. Extraordinary composure under maximum threat.

text_excerpt: I only regret that I have but one life to lose for my country.
label: P1
resistance_rationale: Said immediately before execution as a spy. The value (loyalty to country) is held at the cost of life. No possibility of recantation saving him — pure expression of held value at mortal cost.
source: Last words before hanging, New York, 1776

text_excerpt: Unless I am convinced by the testimony of the Scriptures or by clear reason, I am bound by the Scriptures I have quoted and my conscience is captive to the Word of God. I cannot and will not retract anything, since it is neither safe nor right to go against conscience.
label: P1
resistance_rationale: Said at the Diet of Worms under threat of the same fate as Hus (burned as heretic). Explicit refusal to recant despite real threat of execution. Conscience over safety, explicitly stated.

text_excerpt: I have fought against white domination, and I have fought against black domination. I have cherished the ideal of a democratic and free society in which all persons live together in harmony and with equal opportunities. It is an ideal which I hope to live for and to achieve. But if needs be, it is an ideal for which I am prepared to die.
label: P1
resistance_rationale: Said from the dock facing a sentence that could have been death. Explicitly names the cost he accepts (death) for the value (democratic fairness). No capitulation, no hedging. Resistance is declared, not just implied.

text_excerpt: If slavery is not wrong, nothing is wrong. I cannot remember when I did not so think and feel. And yet I have never understood that the presidency conferred upon me an unrestricted right to act officially upon this judgment and feeling.
label: P1
resistance_rationale: Written during the Civil War when expressing anti-slavery conviction risked political coalition. Lincoln distinguishes personal conviction from official action — the conviction is stated under real political cost. Not mortal stakes but substantial national political risk.

text_excerpt: I prefer to be true to myself, even at the hazard of incurring the ridicule of others, rather than to be false, and to incur my own abhorrence.
label: P1
resistance_rationale: Written by an escaped slave, published knowing he could be recaptured. 'Hazard of incurring the ridicule of others' understates the real risk — but the sentence itself names cost clearly. Authenticity under real personal danger.
source: Narrative of the Life of Frederick Douglass, 1845

text_excerpt: I wrote what I did because as a woman, as a mother, I was oppressed and broken-hearted with the sorrows and injustice I saw, to the best of my ability I have done my duty.
label: P1
resistance_rationale: Written by a woman author in 1853 where public political advocacy by women carried genuine professional and social cost. 'To the best of my ability I have done my duty' signals cost-bearing. Stakes are real but not mortal.

text_excerpt: To be good is noble; but to show others how to be good is nobler and no trouble.
label: P1
resistance_rationale: Casual aphoristic statement made in comfort. No adversity context, no personal cost implied, no pressure. Value is expressed but not demonstrated under resistance. Baseline/floor example for calibration.

text_excerpt: Honesty is the first chapter in the book of wisdom.
label: P1
resistance_rationale: Pure aphorism. No stakes, no adversity, no personal cost implied whatsoever. Aspirational statement made in complete safety. Minimum-resistance floor example.

text_excerpt: I am not bound to win, but I am bound to be true. I am not bound to succeed, but I am bound to live up to what light I have.
label: P1
resistance_rationale: Lincoln states a personal rule with implicit cost ('not bound to win') but no specific adversity context. Written in relative safety. Acknowledges potential failure but doesn't narrate a specific cost being borne.

text_excerpt: When you wake up in the morning, tell yourself: the people I deal with today will be meddling, ungrateful, arrogant, dishonest, jealous, and surly. They are this way because they cannot tell good from evil. But I have seen the beauty of good, and the ugliness of evil, and have recognized that the wrongdoer has a nature related to my own — so none of them can injure me.
label: P1
resistance_rationale: Written as a private discipline (Meditations — private journal). Describes daily adversity context (meddling, ungrateful people) and asserts the value is held regardless. Moderate resistance: the adversity is real and ongoing but not a single crisis. Private document adds authenticity.

text_excerpt: The impediment to action advances action. What stands in the way becomes the way.
label: P1
resistance_rationale: Aphoristic/philosophical statement. Names adversity as a concept ('impediment') but does not describe a specific personal cost being borne. No narrative of personal resistance. Low resistance despite strong language.

text_excerpt: It is not death that a man should fear, but he should fear never beginning to live.
label: P1
resistance_rationale: Philosophical observation about death and fear. Names the value (not fearing death) but is abstract, not descriptive of a moment of personal cost. No adversity in the passage context.

text_excerpt: If you wish to improve, be content to be thought foolish and stupid with regard to external things. Do not wish to seem clever; and if some regard you as important, distrust yourself.
label: P1
resistance_rationale: Epictetus instructs accepting the social cost of seeming foolish as a condition of genuine growth. Moderate resistance: the cost is named (social ridicule) and the instruction is to bear it. Not a narrative of holding under crisis, but a prescribed discipline with real social stakes.

text_excerpt: Seek not the good in external things; seek it in yourself.
label: P1
resistance_rationale: Philosophical instruction without adversity context. Value (independence from external approval) stated as principle, not as a moment of demonstrated cost. Aspirational floor.

text_excerpt: Even in a palace it is possible to live well.
label: P1
resistance_rationale: Quiet statement of philosophical conviction. No adversity, no cost, no pressure described. Low-resistance expression of value principle.

text_excerpt: Let me not be ashamed that my suffering is greater than my courage. I can do what I can. I will not yield in the thing that is right; I can do no more.
label: P1
resistance_rationale: Acknowledges that the personal cost exceeds his comfort ('my suffering is greater than my courage') but explicitly holds ('I will not yield'). The acknowledgment of overwhelming cost while still holding is a very strong resistance signal.

text_excerpt: I will say then that I am not, nor ever have been, in favor of bringing about in any way the social and political equality of the white and black races.
label: P0
resistance_rationale: P0 example at moderate-high resistance. Lincoln made this statement under real political pressure in the 1858 debates in a hostile electoral environment. He compromised the fairness value he privately held (see the 1864 letter) under political cost. Important: high resistance P0 — the cost of holding was real, but the value failed.
source: Fourth Lincoln-Douglas Debate, Charleston IL, September 18, 1858

text_excerpt: I abjure, curse, and detest the aforesaid errors and heresies, and generally all and every error and sect contrary to the Holy Church; and I swear that I will never more in future say or assert anything verbally or in writing which may cause a similar suspicion of me.
label: P0
resistance_rationale: Galileo's formal recantation under threat of torture. This is the canonical high-resistance P0 — the pressure was enormous (Inquisition, threat of torture, imprisonment) and the value (scientific integrity, independence of mind) was abandoned. Critical calibration case: the system must score the pressure as high but recognize the failure.

text_excerpt: And again he denied it with an oath: I do not know the man.
label: P0
resistance_rationale: Peter's second denial. Under real threat (Jesus had just been arrested; Peter could be identified and arrested too). Moderate-high resistance P0: the stakes were genuine arrest, the denial was explicit. The value (loyalty) failed under real pressure.

text_excerpt: I thought it better to shift with the time than to be ruined with the time. It was the safer course.
label: P0
resistance_rationale: Explicit admission that convenience ('safer course') outweighed the commitment. The speaker acknowledges the choice was to yield rather than hold. Moderate resistance: there was real cost to holding but the alternative (yielding) was chosen consciously.

text_excerpt: I gave in because I was tired, because it was not worth the fight, because they would never have understood anyway.
label: P0
resistance_rationale: Low-resistance P0. The value failed but not under severe external pressure — exhaustion and social friction, not mortal stakes. The rationale ('they would never have understood') is rationalization rather than genuine overwhelming force. Failure was chosen, not extracted.

text_excerpt: I never dared to be radical when young for fear that it would make me conservative when old.
label: P0
resistance_rationale: Robert Frost admitting he chose safety (fear of losing conviction) over the value. Low-moderate pressure, no acute crisis — but the admission of chronic capitulation to social fear makes this P0. Value of courage abandoned in small increments over time.
source: Robert Frost, "Ten Miles West of Venus" notebook, 1930s

text_excerpt: Although I had done nothing wrong, I apologized to avoid further trouble. It was the practical thing to do.
label: P0
resistance_rationale: Low-resistance P0. The value (integrity — not falsely admitting fault) failed, but the external pressure was mild ('avoid further trouble'). No mortal stakes. Pure convenience capitulation.

text_excerpt: They threatened to destroy me professionally. They had the power to do it. I knew they were wrong, but I signed the letter anyway.
label: P0
resistance_rationale: High-resistance P0. Professional destruction is a serious real cost. The speaker explicitly knows the right thing ('I knew they were wrong') and explicitly fails ('I signed anyway'). This is the hard case: high resistance, clear knowledge of the right thing, and still fails. Critical training example.

text_excerpt: Every morning I wake up knowing they can come for me today. Every morning I choose to stay anyway, because someone has to say what is true.
label: P1
resistance_rationale: Describes chronic mortal or severe threat ('they can come for me') and active daily choice to hold despite it. The 'every morning' framing indicates sustained adversity, not a single crisis. High resistance: ongoing existential threat, deliberately held.

text_excerpt: I have been advised by my lawyers to say nothing further. But I will say this: I knew what I was doing was right, and I would do it again.
label: P1
resistance_rationale: Legal context implies real legal jeopardy (lawyer present, advised silence). Defies that advice to reaffirm the value. The cost is legal risk. Moderate-high resistance: real stakes, clear defiance, explicit hold statement.
